pattern: Each control character is a tiny editor command, so a single left-to-right fold over the input that mutates an accumulator yields the final text.
hints: Treat the accumulator as a growing buffer and decide, character by character, whether to append, pop, double, or flip it.
Three of the four operations rewrite the whole buffer; only a letter or a removal touches just one end.
Maintain result as a list/string: append letters, drop the last on '*', concatenate it to itself on '#', and reverse it on '%'.

edge_inputs.description: duplicate then append then reverse then pop
removal empties the buffer before a duplicate of nothing
pop on an empty buffer is a no-op
reverse before any letters keeps it empty
repeated duplication grows fast then trims

stress.description: 20-char strings mixing letters and the three operators
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 20)
    pool = list('abcde') + ['*', '#', '%']
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
